text: In the operating room, after induction of general anesthesia, her head was immobilized using skull clamp and angled for near orthogonal approach to the tumor.
Using computer navigation, the precise location of the tumor, craniotomy perimeter, as well as planned incision were marked on the scalp.
Now you're looking through a view through the surgical microscope.
After making the incision, the location of the superior sagittal sinus and adjacent craniotomy were marked on the skull.
bare hole was placed over the lateral edge of the sinus and the bone flap was elevated utilizing a side cutting drill.

In this case, we were mindful of the nature of the tumor and the possibility of its adherence, if not invasion of the skull, and therefore the bone flap was raised carefully with the tumor attached to it.
The tumor had left an impression on the brain by deforming it.
Usually most of this deformation resolves once the tumor is removed.
Brain tumor invading the skull is shown here.
This tumor was separated from the bone flap and the bone flap was cleared from all tumor material using high-speed diamond bit drill.
Due to benign nature of the tumor and the fact that it was removed completely with no residual tumor, patient did not require radiation treatment or chemotherapy after the operation.
